title: x-emilia-action — per-operation action-consequence declaration
description: The inline, per-endpoint form of an EMILIA agent-action-control declaration, for use as an OpenAPI vendor extension (x-emilia-action) on an operation object. Same semantics as one entry of /.well-known/agent-action-control.json: it declares what a consequential operation does (effects) and what authorization evidence it requires (authority), so an agent runtime can weigh consequences and satisfy the requirement before acting. The effects block is ADVISORY; the authority/enforcement requirement is the control. Aligns with draft-schrock-agent-action-manifest and EP-ACTION-TYPE-PROFILE-REGISTRY.

action.description: Canonical action-type URN, urn:ep:action:<family>.<action>, from the EP action-type registry (e.g. urn:ep:action:finance.wire_transfer). Owning the vocabulary is the point: this is the stable name evidence binds to and auditors query.

effects.description: ADVISORY preview of what the operation does. Never a control; never lowers the authority requirement.

authority.description: The CONTROL: what authorization evidence is required before the effect boundary. Fail-closed at the enforcement point; this declaration is discoverable, not the enforcement itself.

execution_binding.description: Material fields that MUST be observed from the system of record and match the authorized action (stops parameter drift between approval and execution).

evidence.description: Evidence the enforcement point emits after the effect boundary — the correlation anchors a runtime/auditor threads together.

rollback.description: Whether the effect can be programmatically undone, and where. Advisory, paired with effects.reversibility.

operation_id.description: the OpenAPI operationId (or URN) that reverses this action, when supported
